findings.text: The calibration error improves materially while accuracy remains nearly unchanged.
Calibration uncertainty and comparison with another calibrator are missing.
Median latency falls substantially at nearly equal recall.
The evaluation omits scale, hardware diversity, and invalidation behavior.
The diagnostic yields inspectable flagged cases rather than only an aggregate error.
Selecting the threshold on the evaluation set risks optimistic flagging.
The distilled student improves the reported token accuracy at equal inference latency.
A single seed cannot establish that the 1.7-point gain is stable.
Prioritization finds many more injected errors than random ordering at the same budget.
Independent synthetic flips may not model systematic annotation errors.
The masked method improves error under four missing sensors and reports uncertainty.
Independent masks do not test correlated sensor failures.
The mechanism states sensitivity, epsilon, and the neighboring relation.
Repeated-release composition is absent, limiting the practical privacy claim.
Bias improves on all three generated graph families.
Test-informed depth selection makes the reported comparison optimistic.
Importance-based deletion preserves average accuracy better than random deletion.
The average hides a large drop for the lowest-resource language.
Trace users recover substantially more exact configurations than note users.
The three-person, unblinded single-organization study has weak external validity.
